response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with a long career in web, backend, and systems work, but he does not meet the role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience is roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++. He lists no SQL Server or Oracle experience (MongoDB/MySQL instead), and has no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain background — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets. The gap between his profile and the core requirements is too large to justify an interview for this specific engagement.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and courses he created and taught
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015), versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB and MySQL
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for mandatory requirement
Missing capital markets / fixed income domain experience
Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
